stage-1-film-filters: Load the diffusion model and apply LoRA modifiers. LoRAs adjust the model's style — Detail controls fine-grain sharpness, Chiaroscuro shifts lighting contrast. These filters shape every image produced downstream.
stage-2-subject-style: Describe the subject and choose the film format (aspect ratio). The positive prompt is encoded into CLIP embeddings that guide the diffusion process. Film format sets the latent image dimensions.
stage-3-develop-contact: Run a quick 6-step diffusion pass to produce a contact print — a fast preview for evaluating composition and prompt adherence before committing to longer refinement stages.
stage-4-work-print: Latent-upscale the contact print (default 1.5×) and run a second 4-step diffusion pass, then sharpen. Adds detail and resolution. ComfyUI caches earlier stages so only this work is computed.
stage-5-final-print: AI upscale with SeedVR2 (7B) plus final sharpening. Produces the highest-resolution output. Can skip the Work Print stage and upscale directly from the contact print.
